stories.title: Monday briefing: Will the international effort to stop the further spread of hantavirus be successful?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: ​The outbreak aboard a luxury cruise ship​ offers a stark reminder of how easily a deadly pathogen can hitch a ride across borders​, and how fragile global preparedness remains Good morning. This is Michael Segalov – as of today, I’ll be popping up into your inboxes from time to time. There’ll be no shortage of infighting, intrigue and briefing in Westminster this week, as the dust settles on the government’s disastrous election results. We’ll be bringing you the latest on

stories.title: EU’s Kallas rejects Gerhard Schröder as Russia-Ukraine negotiator
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Former German Chancellor Gerhard Schröder is unfit to represent Europe in any peace talks to end the Russia-Ukraine war, the EU’s chief diplomat Kaja Kallas said Monday. Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested over the weekend that Schröder, who was German chancellor from 1998 to 2005, could represent European countries in peace talks. Schröder previously held roles with Russian state-owned energy companies. “First, if we give the right to Russia to appoint a negoti

stories.title: EU’s Kallas criticises Putin’s ‘very cynical’ Ukraine ceasefire calls and rejects suggestion of Schröder as mediator – Europe live
stories.summary: EU’s foreign policy chief and the ex-Estonian PM dismiss Putin’s suggestion that former German chancellor could coordinate talks Estonian foreign minister Margus Tsahkna also dismissed Putin’s suggestion that former German chancellor Gerhard Schröder could coordinate talks with the European Union to secure a peace deal in Ukraine. “Gerhard Schröder is a Putin idea. I think they are very close. Gerhard Schröder won’t be representing Europe, ” said Tsahkna, as he arrived for an EU meeting in Bruss

stories.title: EU to approve sanctions on Israeli settlers, says Kallas
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The EU is poised to approve sanctions against so-called violent Israeli settlers, after Hungary’s new government signaled it would no longer block the measure, top diplomat Kaja Kallas said Monday ahead of the Foreign Affairs Council in Brussels. As first reported in Playbook , ministers are expected to reach a political agreement on the long-stalled sanctions, which former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán had repeatedly blocked . But a breakthrough now appears possible afte

stories.title: US Senate expected to confirm Kevin Warsh as next Federal Reserve chair
stories.summary: Warsh would succeed outgoing Fed chair Jerome Powell as Trump continues his push to influence the US central bank Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The US Senate is expected to confirm Kevin Warsh this week as chair of the Federal Reserve , as Donald Trump continues his campaign to influence the world’s most important central bank. The Fed’s influence over the economy spans from the job market to mortgage rates, and its every move is carefully scrutinized by investors on Wall Str

stories.title: Ziobro confirms he is in US, says he travelled there with document issued by Hungary
stories.summary: The former Polish Minister of Justice Minister is wanted at home on embezzlement charges and had previously been granted asylum in Hungary.

stories.title: Hungary’s next health minister Zsolt Hegedűs dances at Magyar celebration
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered outside the Hungarian parliament building to celebrate the official inauguration of Peter Magyar as prime minister. Attendees included incoming Health Minister Zsolt Hegedus, who went viral for his dancing on the night of the country's April election.

stories.title: Szinte az egész csapat egy városban született, mégis legyűrték Európa nagyágyúit
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. BL-sztorik, ötödik rész.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt a szívhangrendeletről: A szakmát megerőszakolta a politika
stories.summary: A miniszterjelöltet az Egészségügyi Bizottság parlamenti ülésén hallgatják meg. Népegészségügyi államtitkárságot hoznak létre, 80 évre emelik a születéskor várható élettartamot, és átlátható betegvisszajelzést ígérnek. Hegedűs Zsolt bejelentette, hogy Svéd Tamás, a MOK főtitkára politikai államtitkár lesz.

stories.title: Orbán Anita: A vétó nem lesz többet kampányeszköz
stories.summary: A leendő külügyminiszter a parlament EU-ügyi bizottsági meghallgatásán úgy látta, túl gyakran voltunk a küllők között, és túl ritkán a kerék küllője.

stories.title: Bóna Szabolcs: Nem dolgozzuk fel a Magyarországon termelt élelmiszer nagy részét, ezen változtatnunk kell
stories.summary: A Tisza agrárminiszter-jelöltje a klímaváltozás kihívásairól és a fiatalok lehetőséghez juttatásáról is beszélt parlamenti meghallgatásán. A bizottság megszavazta, hogy ő vezesse a mezőgazdasági tárcát.

stories.title: Svéd Tamás, az orvosi kamara eddigi főtitkára lesz a politikai államtitkár a Hegedűs Zsolt által vezetett egészségügyi minisztériumban
stories.summary: A miniszteri posztra jelölt Hegedűs Zsolt bizottsági meghallgatásán jelentette be ezt. A Nobel-díjas Karikó Katalin is részt vesz a miniszter mellett felálló tanácsadó testületben.

stories.title: Két román férfi rendőrnek kiadva magát vette el egy külföldi kamionsofőr pénzét Lajosmizsén
stories.summary: A férfiakat a rendőrök elfogták, őrizetbe is vették őket.

stories.title: Alternatív különdíjat kapott a tánc népszerűsítéséért Hegedűs Zsolt
stories.summary: A Lábán Rudolf-díj a legfontosabb elismerés, amit magyar színházi kortárstánc-előadásokért adnak.

stories.title: Orbán Anita: Szuverén külpolitika nélkül nincs szuverén ország
stories.summary: A Szent István-i államalapítás örökségéhez való igazodást és a magyar diplomácia megújítását ígérte a külügyminiszter-jelölt a Külügyi Bizottság hétfői meghallgatásán.

stories.title: Itt a Szájkarate 3.0, a választási kampány legbizarrabb történetét keressük
stories.summary: Egy hónap telt el a választás óta, itt az ideje, hogy történelmi távlatból vizsgáljuk meg a 21. századi Magyarország talán legőrültebb kampányidőszakát. Mi volt a legbizarrabb jelenség, a legabszurdabb pillanat, miből lesz történelem érettségi tétel 50 év múlva? Ön dönt! Az első fordulóban: kétarcús képregény Gundalf ellen.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a Facebookon kérdezi a követőit Sulyok Tamás lemondásáról
stories.summary: A bejegyzésre két óra alatt közel 150 ezer lájk érkezett.

stories.title: Trump open to shifting US troops from Germany to Poland
stories.summary: Will US troops withdrawn from Germany be moved to Poland? When asked, US President Donald Trump said Warsaw favours the idea, and it is feasible.

stories.title: Lemondott a lett védelmi miniszter, miután két drón zuhant csütörtökön az országra
stories.summary: A drónok Ukrajnához tartoztak, és az ukrán külügyminiszter szerint eltérítették őket.

stories.title: Bajnok lett Varga Barnabás az AEK Athénnal
stories.summary: Varga idén januárban igazolt a Fradiból a görög csapathoz.

stories.title: A magyar TikTok szexikonjai már nem celebek, hanem politikusok
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt politikusaiból szexszimbólumok lettek a TikTokon. Nem csak Magyarról és Radnairól, már Velkey György Lászlóról is nyálcsorgatós videók készülnek, Orbán Anitát és Gajdos Lászlót viszont inkább a kedvességükért szeretik. Médiakutatóval vizsgáltuk meg a jelenséget.

stories.title: Ziobro megerősítette, hogy Amerikában van, a Magyarországtól kapott okmányával utazott oda
stories.summary: Az előző kormány adott menedékjogot a Lengyelországban körözés alatt álló exminiszternek, Varsó magyarázatot vár az amerikai és magyar felektől.

stories.title: A hosszú Covid szindrómás szerző a zavarodottság mesterművét próbálta megírni
stories.summary: Patricia Lockwood lapozva úgy érezhetjük, mintha Kassák Lajos nikkelszamovárja repülne el felettünk egy kortárs amerikai író regényébe csomagolva.

stories.title: Hungary: New govt intent on initiating swift systemic change
stories.summary: Peter Magyar and his government are determined to push ahead with change at full throttle. But even before the cabinet is sworn in, there has been a "resignation." And questions remain about Magyar's Ukraine policy.

stories.title: "We've lived with them for 80 years": Inside a German town that US troops could soon leave after Donald Trump's withdrawal threat
stories.summary: If US President Donald Trump is serious, 5,000 US soldiers could be withdrawn from Germany. This would include the Stryker Brigade in Vilseck. The town is in shock.

stories.title: Hungary's new government pushes for euro by 2030
stories.summary: Hungary's new leadership wants the country to adopt the euro by the end of the decade while repairing strained ties with Brussels. But with a weak economy and tight deadlines, experts warn the path will be steep.

stories.title: Romania's government falls, far-right firewall crumbles
stories.summary: Romania's Social Democrats have joined forces with the far right to oust the pro-reform government of Ilie Bolojan. The economic impact will be serious; the political cost high. Is the far right on the brink of power?

stories.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire frays
stories.summary: US president expresses ire at Tehran’s reported demands, as drones strike Gulf nations and Israel warns war ‘not over’ Donald Trump has rejected an Iranian response to a US peace proposal as “totally unacceptable”, on a day the month-old ceasefire showed signs of fraying as drone strikes were reported around the region and Benjamin Netanyahu warned the war was “not over”. The Iranian counter-proposal was passed to Washington through Pakistani mediators. Continue reading...

stories.title: Greece scrambles to explain how explosive-packed drone landed in its waters
stories.summary: Minister defends preparedness against novel threat after officials detonate device suspected to be from Ukraine Authorities in Greece have intensified investigations into how an explosive-packed drone ended up in waters off the west of the country. An inquiry, involving specialised military teams, broadened at the weekend after bomb disposal experts detonated the unmanned device at sea. Continue reading...

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump rejects Iran response to US peace proposal as Tehran warns of new attacks
stories.summary: US president calls Iranian response ‘totally unacceptable’ while Tehran says it will retaliate against any new US strikes or foreign warships in strait of Hormuz Trump calls Iran’s response to peace plan ‘totally unacceptable’ as ceasefire frays Iranian authorities on Monday hanged a postgraduate student from a university in Tehran on charges of espionage. Erfan Shakourzadeh, 29, was hanged after being convicted for collaborating with the CIA and the Mossad, the Iranian judiciary’s Mizan Online

stories.title: Germany news: Defense minister Pistorius arrives in Kyiv
stories.summary: Pistorius plans to discuss building a new weapons system with Ukraine in the unannounced visit. Meanwhile in Stuttgart, five pro-Palestinian activists face trial over attacking an Israeli defense company site. Follow DW.

stories.title: RSF urges Niger to ditch 'abusive' French media ban
stories.summary: Media watchdog Reporters Without Borders has called on Niger's Russian-backed military government to lift its sudden suspension of nine French media outlets. This coincides with major instability in neighboring Mali.

stories.title: Europe should behave more like China does if it wants to survive this age of chaos
stories.summary: Instead of obsessing over rules that have ceased to matter, we must consider giving Beijing a dose of its own medicine The US and Israel may have started the war in Iran, but – apart from the belligerents themselves – it is China and Europe that stand to lose the most from it. Yet while European leaders watch like rabbits caught in the headlights as energy prices shoot through the roof, China has responded to the crisis with remarkable equanimity. It is striking how self-confident Beijing is ahe

stories.title: My Father’s Diaries review – haunting home-video excavates trauma of Srebrenica massacre
stories.summary: Ado Hasanović’s moving documentary transforms footage filmed during the Bosnian war into a devastating portrait of memory, survival and inherited grief For years, film-maker Ado Hasanović has wanted to ask his father, Bekir, about his harrowing experiences during the Bosnian war but their conversations usually ended with curt, abrupt answers that obscured rather than illuminated the past. Bekir might be uncommunicative, but his collection of self-taped films and diary entries recorded during the

stories.title: Starmer faces fight to survive as Streeting and Rayner eye leadership bids
stories.summary: Chances of Starmer remaining in No 10 appear to be diminishing as about 40 Labour MPs call on him to quit UK politics live – latest updates Who are the main threats to Keir Starmer’s Labour leadership? Keir Starmer faces a fight for his political life in the next 24 hours as potential Labour leadership rivals, from Wes Streeting to Angela Rayner, begin positioning themselves for a contest. Starmer is hoping to save his job on Monday with a speech promising to “face up to the big challenges” for

stories.title: Body ‘in decomposed state’ believed to be Julian Ingram – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast ‘We’re not a party of government … yet,’ says Joyce Barnaby Joyce is doing something of a victory lap for One Nation this morning, hitting the radio and TVs to celebrate the emphatic byelection win. The sort of conceited statement, ‘Oh, you’re not a party of government.’ Well, let’s finish that with a rejoiner, yet. This is a dynamic change. And talking to people last night in the western suburbs of Sydney, last

stories.title: Keir Starmer says ‘I will prove my doubters wrong’ as he tries to avert leadership challenge – UK politics live
stories.summary: PM says government will bring forward legislation this week to nationalise British Steel during his make-or-break speech Botterill says voters she spoke to during the campaign felt the country does not work for them. She is a working-class Yorkshire woman, she says. She knows that the opportunities she has enjoyed would not be there if if had not been for the achievements of Labour government. She says Labour is one of the best vehicles for changing the lives of working people that this county h

stories.title: Oil jumps 4% as Trump rejects Iran’s response to ceasefire proposal
stories.summary: Oil prices jumped on Monday morning while European markets edged lower and Asian stocks rose to new all time highs after US President Donald Trump rejected Tehran’s response to the latest proposal on ending the war in Iran.

stories.title: Philippine VP Sara Duterte impeached for a second time
stories.summary: If convicted in a Senate trial, which is uncertain, she will be disqualified from running for president.

stories.title: Starmer critic sets Tuesday deadline for leadership challenge
stories.summary: LONDON — A Labour former minister threatening to challenge Keir Starmer as prime minister will give MPs until Tuesday morning to nominate her as leader. Catherine West said Saturday she plans to force a leadership contest after last week’s devastating local election results for the ruling Labour Party, unless Starmer signals a change of direction in a speech Monday, or a Cabinet minister launches a challenge. West, a former Foreign Office minister, told London Playbook : “If I feel it’s fl

stories.title: Martin Short opens up about ‘nightmare’ death of his daughter Katherine
stories.summary: Actor and comedian speaks publicly for the first time since his 42-year-old daughter died by suicide in February Martin Short has spoken for the first time about the death of his daughter, Katherine Short, saying her death has been “a nightmare for the family”. Katherine died in February aged 42, at her home in the Hollywood Hills. The County of Los Angeles Medical Examiner’s office confirmed she died by suicide. In the US, you can call or text the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline at 988 or cha

stories.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers 'remain asymptomatic' before flights home – video
stories.summary: Spanish health minister Monica Garcia says all those onboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were 'asymptomatic' after it arrived in Tenerife in the Canary Islands on Sunday. Everyone disembarking will be checked for symptoms, authorities say, and be taken off the ship only once evacuation flights are ready to fly them to their destinations Argentina in spotlight over hantavirus as authorities retrace footsteps of ship’s passengers Dozens of people from cruise ship struck by hantavirus leave T

stories.title: Get back: Beatles’ Apple Corps to turn former London base into seven-storey visitor attraction
stories.summary: Paul McCartney hails plans for 3 Savile Row, which include access to rooftop where the band played their final gig The address may not sound familiar, and the street name is best known as the heart of British tailoring. But 3 Savile Row is one of the most iconic buildings in British pop and rock: the former home of the Beatles’ record label Apple Corps, and the location of the band’s final public performance when they took to its rooftop in 1969. Apple Corps has now re-acquired the building in M

stories.title: Britain and Ireland’s wildflowers – in pictures
stories.summary: The Eden Project’s National Wildflower Centre is opening entries for its Wildflower Photographer of the Year 2026 competition on 29 May. The contest showcases images of some of Britain and Ireland’s 1,600 wildflower species, and a selection of photos from last year’s competition will be on display at Eden Dock, Canary Wharf, London, during CWG’s Nature Week, from 13 July Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘It was either this or the pool’: hantavirus ship becomes latest Tenerife tourist attraction
stories.summary: The MV Hondius, the cruise ship hit by an outbreak of a virus that has left three dead, reached the Canary Islands on Sunday On a dusty hill overlooking the Atlantic Ocean in the south of Tenerife, groups of tourists and locals are gathered to witness one of the island’s best new attractions. Some are gazing through binoculars while others are taking photos on their phones of a vessel only a few hundreds metres away, anchored near the Granadillo commercial port. Continue reading...

stories.title: Heathrow passenger numbers dip as demand for international travel ebbs amid Iran war fallout
stories.summary: Airport reports 5% decline but transit passenger business rises as fliers use hub to avoid Middle East airports Business live – news updates The number of passengers flying from London Heathrow fell last month, as war in the Middle East weighed on demand for international travel. About 6.7 million people flew through the airport in April, a 5% drop compared with the same period in 2025 and its biggest annual fall since March last year. Continue reading...

stories.title: Betting account on 18th birthday, dead at 22: inquest probes death of Melbourne man who gambled $895,000
stories.summary: Coroner examines circumstances that led to 2021 death of Werribee man Kyle Hudson, who held multiple betting accounts Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Melbourne man gambled $895,733 through online betting platforms in the four years between his 18th birthday and the day he took his own life, an inquest has heard. Victorian coroner Paul Lawrie’s inquest into the circumstances of Kyle Hudson’s death on 6 July 2021 began on Monday, and will interrogate whether betting

stories.title: No butts: case dismissed after Woolworths worker claims hurt feelings over plumber’s crack
stories.summary: Fair Work Commission deputy president Alan Colman rejects Victorian man’s case, warning there is no disincentive for speculative claims Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A former Woolworths employee has been accused of wasting the Fair Work Commission’s time for filing an unfair dismissal case over feeling “upset” at being told to cover up his bum crack. In a decision published on Thursday, the Fair Work Commission

stories.title: Weather tracker: US and Mexico brace for heatwave as deadly floods hit South Africa
stories.summary: Temperatures soar in California and Arizona, while deluge continues across Western and Northern Cape Heat is expected to intensify across western parts of the US and Mexico this week as a ridge of high pressure pushes temperatures well above the seasonal norm. Daytime highs are forecast to reach 10-15C above average in some areas. The US National Weather Service has issued heat advisories for parts of California and Arizona, with extreme heat warnings in force on Monday and Tuesday in places suc

stories.title: Body believed to be alleged triple murderer Julian Ingram found near abandoned ute in ‘decomposed state’
stories.summary: Ingram is accused of killing Sophie Quinn, her new boyfriend and her aunt in Lake Cargelligo, about 450km west of Sydney Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A body found in a “decomposed state” next to an abandoned ute 50km north-west of Lake Cargelligo is believed to be a gunman suspected of killing his pregnant former partner in remote New South Wales . A large-scale manhunt for Julian Ingram, 37, has been under way since January when he allegedly shot and killed Sophi

stories.title: Portrait looted by Nazis found in home of Dutch SS leader’s family
stories.summary: Toon Kelder artwork from famed Goudstikker collection resurfaces with descendants of Hendrik Seyffardt Europe live – latest updates An artwork looted by the Nazis from the renowned Goudstikker collection has resurfaced in the home of descendants of a notorious Dutch SS collaborator, according to an art detective. Portrait of a Young Girl, by the Dutch artist Toon Kelder, is believed to have hung for decades in the home of Hendrik Seyffardt’s family, Arthur Brand said, describing it as “the most

stories.title: Can you move your pension to dodge inheritance tax? Fraudsters say so
stories.summary: Criminals exploit confusion or anxiety over new IHT rules by offering a ‘safe haven’ for savings pots The caller pitches a great deal. Shift the moneysaved in your pension and reinvest it in a scheme overseas where you can avoid it being caught under next year’s changes to the UK’s inheritance tax (IHT) system . From April next year, any money left in a defined contribution pension after your death, which is most workplace and all private pensions, will be pulled into the IHT net. Continue readi

stories.title: Evacuated US and French MV Hondius passengers test positive for hantavirus
stories.summary: French woman taken to Paris in serious condition while American flown to Nebraska is asymptomatic, say officials Europe live – latest updates A French woman and an American national evacuated from the cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak have tested positive for the virus, as the complex operation to repatriate those on board continued on Monday. The French woman was one of five French passengers who disembarked from the ship in Tenerife on Sunday before being flown to a hos

stories.title: Man charged after allegedly threatening Muslim worshippers at Brisbane mosque
stories.summary: According to the Australian National Imams Council, a man entered Masjid Taqwa mosque claiming to have an AK-47 firearm in his vehicle Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s peak Islamic body has condemned growing “anti-Muslim sentiment”, after a man allegedly threatened worshippers at a Brisbane mosque on Sunday, falsely claiming to have a gun. The man is alleged to have attended the Masjid Taqwa in Bald Hills, Brisbane at about 10.46am on Sunday and threatened

stories.title: ‘Amazon of America’: film paints vision of a post-coup Brazil giving up rainforest
stories.summary: Vitória Régia imagines rightwing Bolsonaro plot succeeded with US help – and highlights threats facing Indigenous peoples The year is 2025 and far-right coup plotters have annihilated Brazil’s democracy, assassinating the president, closing the national congress and surrendering the Amazon rainforest and its untold riches to the United States. “Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the Amazon of America,” a thick-accented North American soldier tells a group of journalists being taken on a propaganda

stories.title: Angela Rayner calls blocking Andy Burnham’s return to parliament a mistake as pressure mounts on Keir Starmer – as it happened
stories.summary: Catherine West issues ultimatum for PM as ex-minister Josh Simons joins calls for prime minister to quit At the start of her programme Laura Kuenssberg addressed Catherine West and Bridget Phillipson who were sitting waiting for the main interviews. Kuenssberg told West she wanted a cabinet minister to challenge Keir Starmer. She said she was sitting next to one of them. What was her message to her? Well, there’s nothing stopping Bridget from standing. Why are all the men better than the women?

stories.title: Iranian Nobel winner Mohammadi moved to Tehran hospital
stories.summary: Human rights activist and Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been released on bail and transferred to Tehran, her foundation said, amid alarm about her health.

stories.title: Former Thai Prime Minister Thaksin let out of prison early
stories.summary: Thailand's influential former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra will be required to wear an electronic monitor during his four-month probation period.

stories.title: Why is Hollywood missing from Cannes?
stories.summary: There are no US blockbusters premiering at the Cannes Film Festival this year. So who's snubbing who?

stories.title: Germany's business community is frustrated and disillusioned
stories.summary: No one had higher hopes for Friedrich Merz's chancellorship than the German business community. Meanwhile, however, early euphoria has given way to bitter disappointment.

stories.title: India news: Cut fuel use, gold buys and foreign trips, says Modi, as no end in sight to Iran war
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is asking Indians to make a series of changes in their consumption habits, also including foreign trips and buying gold jewelry. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Hantavirus: Evacuated passengers begin returning home
stories.summary: Passengers on the ill-fated cruise from several European countries, including Germany, are returning home and will be screened by health authorities. One French woman developed symptoms on the return flight. Follow DW.

stories.title: Somali piracy adds new strain to global shipping and trade routes
stories.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

stories.title: Nazi-looted artwork surfaces with Dutch collaborator's heirs
stories.summary: A Nazi-looted painting from a famed Dutch collection has surfaced in the home of decendents of a senior Nazi collaborator. The painting’s wherabouts became known after a relative discovered the truth.

stories.title: Syria: Court charges Bashar Assad's cousin Atef Najib with war crimes amid 2011 uprising
stories.summary: Syria's judiciary has charged Atef Najib with acts "amounting to war crimes" as Bashar Assad's cousin is accused of orchestrating a crackdown on 2011 protests that subsequently degenerated into a decade of civil war.

stories.title: Why Eurovision's fallout over Israel may change the competition forever
stories.summary: With Eurovision facing its biggest boycott in 70 years, questions are growing over the competition's future.

stories.title: Ailing Iran Nobel laureate given bail and hospital transfer
stories.summary: Iranian authorities granted human rights activist Narges Mohammadi bail amid growing concern over her poor health in prison.

stories.title: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
stories.summary: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

stories.title: Dua Lipa sues Samsung for $15m over use of her image on TV boxes
stories.summary: The pop star's legal team alleges a photograph of her face was used on Samsung television boxes without permission.

stories.title: French national shows symptoms on return from hantavirus-hit ship
stories.summary: Five passengers of the MV Hondius will be quarantined in Paris "until further notice", France's prime minister says.

stories.title: Thailand's divisive ex-PM is out of jail, but is the Thaksin era over?
stories.summary: Thaksin seems incapable of taking a back seat. This time, it really could be different.

stories.title: Trump to discuss Iran war and trade on first US presidential visit to China since 2017
stories.summary: The US leader was expected to travel to China earlier this year, but his trip was postponed due to the Iran war. It marks the first visit by a US president to China since 2017.

stories.title: Venice Biennale performances confront war and climate fears
stories.summary: Artists at the Venice Biennale used striking performances and immersive installations to explore themes including war, caregiving and human fragility.

stories.title: Modi urges Indians to work from home and limit foreign travel as Iran war continues
stories.summary: He said the austerity measures would reduce India's fuel use and help save foreign exchange.

stories.title: Eurovision 2026 kicks off with turquoise carpet parade in Vienna
stories.summary: Images show large crowds gather to welcome contestants for the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest on the Turquoise Carpet in Vienna.

stories.title: BBC tours England and Argentina's World Cup training grounds
stories.summary: Will Grant tested out one of the pitches where England captain Harry Kane and his team will train in Kansas City.

stories.title: Remains of US soldier who went missing during military drill in Morocco recovered
stories.summary: The disappearances triggered a search-and-rescue operation involving more than 600 personnel from the United States, Morocco and other military partners.

stories.title: Iran executes young aerospace engineer over CIA and Mossad espionage allegations
stories.summary: Iran has executed a 29-year-old aerospace engineer Erfan Shakourzadeh on Monday for alleged spying, despite his claims of torture and coerced confession, according to his note from prison.

stories.title: Vienna unveils giant Eurovision mural
stories.summary: Vienna has unveiled Austria's largest mural on a vacant office tower as part of the Eurovision Song Contest cultural programme.

stories.title: Hundreds of ships mark Hamburg harbour’s 837th anniversary
stories.summary: Hamburg celebrated the 837th anniversary of its harbour with a maritime parade featuring around 250 ships, from historic sailing vessels to modern coast guard boats.

stories.title: Oil prices jump after Trump dismisses Iran proposal to end war
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz waterway remains effectively shut, severely disrupting global energy shipments.

stories.title: US and French nationals test positive for hantavirus after leaving ship
stories.summary: The American national has arrived in Nebraska, while a French woman is isolating in Paris.

stories.title: Turkish Airlines plane evacuated after landing gear fire in Nepal, officials say
stories.summary: The Himalayan nation is home to some of the world's most remote and tricky runways, flanked by snow-capped peaks and terrain that poses a challenge even for accomplished pilots.

stories.title: Trump's China visit set to test fragile truce
stories.summary: The first US presidential visit to China in almost 10 years will test a fragile tariff truce.

stories.title: Iran warns Europe against sending warships to Hormuz as US talks gap widens
stories.summary: Tehran has warned Europe against the idea of sending warships to the key waterway, as it outlined a conditional proposal on uranium, sanctions relief and a Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.title: Report: Iraqi shepherd uncovers alleged secret Israeli base in desert
stories.summary: An intelligence expert said the desert in western Iraq is an ideal location for such secret bases, given its size and sparse population.

stories.title: Rewiring the Financial System for Growth
stories.summary: An event by MFA. In the year since the Government’s flagship Leeds Reforms and Financial Services Growth and Competitiveness Plan, what progress has been made in rewiring the UK’s financial system for a much-needed boost to growth? This event brings together key figures from Westminster, the financial regulators and the financial services industry to consider the impact of the Government’s financial services agenda for the country’s economic growth prospects. Guiding questions to be addressed in

stories.title: Putin says Russia will prevail at scaled-down Victory Day parade – video
stories.summary: Russia held its smallest Victory Day parade in years on Saturday due to security concerns over Ukraine drone strikes. The parade on Red Square marks the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany and was once an opportunity to show off Russia's vast military hardware and nuclear missiles, but this year there were no tanks rolling over the cobbles of the Moscow square Russia will always be victorious, says Putin at scaled-back Victory Day parade Continue reading...

stories.title: Greek minister says mystery drone from a 'foreign state'
stories.summary: A Greek military source said the flying robot, found this week off the island of Lefkada, may have been Ukrainian. Kyiv has admitted using drones to target Russian oil tankers in the Mediterranean.

stories.title: Saudi Aramco profits jump despite conflict in Middle East
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s state oil company’s profits up 26% to £26.9bn in first three months of year Business live – latest updates Saudi Arabia’s state oil company reported a 26% jump in profits in its first quarter as its east-west pipeline allowed it to ship millions of barrels of oil out of the Gulf despite conflict in the Middle East . Profits at Saudi Aramco hit $33.6bn (£26.9bn) in the first three months of the year, while revenue rose nearly 7% compared with a year earlier to $115.5bn. Continue re

stories.title: Research sheds light on GI’s murder of seven-year-old girl in Northern Ireland in 1944
stories.summary: William Harrison, a US soldier stationed in the region, was convicted and hanged for the murder of Patsy Wylie On the afternoon of 25 September 1944, William Harrison, a US soldier stationed in Northern Ireland , visited the cottage of the Wylie family in Killycolpy, County Tyrone, and offered to buy treats for the children. He had visited before and was, if not a friend, at least known to the family. Mary Wylie let him take her seven-year-old daughter, Patricia, better known as Patsy, across th

stories.title: Health advocates warn government’s claims of baby formula safety contradict data
stories.summary: Independent scientists who reviewed the results said most samples were contaminated with Pfas or phthalates The Trump administration announced earlier this month that hundreds of baby formula samples it tested for toxic chemicals “meet a high safety standard”, but public health advocates warn this claim contradicts data showing a majority were contaminated with dangerous substances, such as Pfas or phthalates. Independent scientists who reviewed the results say the data gaps and the contaminatio

stories.title: Body of missing US soldier recovered off coast of Morocco
stories.summary: 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr was participating in largest military exercise among US, Nato allies and African nations A search team recovered the body of a US soldier who went missing near a cliff during a training exercise in Cap Draa, Morocco, the US Army said on Sunday. Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1.6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Nobel laureate’s smuggled memoir details beatings and neglect in Iranian prisons
stories.summary: Writing by Narges Mohammadi, arrested 14 times for activism, offers a disturbing insight into treatment In an exclusive extract of writing smuggled from prison in Iran, the Nobel peace prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has described the “torture” of solitary confinement, and her systematic medical neglect by the prison system. The writing from the past decade will be part of a soon to be published memoir that gives a rare and alarming insight into the treatment of Mohammadi, who is in critical con

stories.title: Berlin: Fighting the city's young far-right scene
stories.summary: A recent police raid on Germany's neo-Nazi scene has revealed how alarmingly young some of its members are. But those targeted by this hatred are fighting back.

stories.title: 'No comment' after Schröder named by Putin for Ukraine talks
stories.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested that the former German chancellor serve as a mediator in Russia-Ukraine peace talks.

stories.title: France turns to Africa for strategic partnerships
stories.summary: Following a withdrawal from the Sahel region, France is seeking to expand its geostrategic influence in Africa by seeking new allies. The first Africa Summit in Kenya is intended to mark a fresh start.

stories.title: Germany: 'Ulm 5' on trial after attack on Israeli arms firm
stories.summary: Five activists have gone on trial in Germany following a break-in at the Israeli arms company Elbit Systems in Ulm. Their lawyers intend to argue they were trying to obstruct a genocide.

stories.title: Could German ex-leader really negotiate Ukraine peace?
stories.summary: Former German chancellor Gerhard Schröder is a controversial — and some critics would even say, unserious — choice as a negotiator on the Ukraine war. He's long been criticized for being too close to Russia.

stories.title: Iran war: Trump rejects proposal to end conflict
stories.summary: Donald Trump has called Iran's plan "totally unacceptable." The fragile ceasefire had been tested earlier in the day by Iranian strikes across the Gulf. DW has more.

stories.title: Watch: Passengers evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
stories.summary: Passengers from the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius are being evacuated in Tenerife and transferred by bus to quarantine facilities and the airport.

stories.title: Trump rejects Iranian response to US ceasefire proposal as 'totally unacceptable'
stories.summary: Iran had responded to Washington's latest peace proposal earlier in the day, while warning it would not hold back from retaliating against any new US strikes or permit more foreign warships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Putin says he thinks Ukraine conflict 'coming to an end'
stories.summary: The Russian leader sees potential for Ukraine negotiations, but condemns the West's backing for President Zelensky.

stories.title: Australia's right-wing One Nation party scores historic parliamentary win
stories.summary: One Nation gains its first seat in the lower house, in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist party.

stories.title: Army parachutes onto remote island to help Briton with suspected hantavirus
stories.summary: The specialist team parachuted onto Tristan da Cunha, a remote British overseas territory, to treat them.

stories.title: UK Army parachutes onto remote Atlantic island over suspected hantavirus case
stories.summary: The British military parachuted medics and emergency supplies into Tristan da Cunha after a resident was suspected of contracting hantavirus.

stories.title: How hotels are stopping the 'dawn dash' for sunbeds after man wins payout
stories.summary: Some resorts enforce allocation rules from check-in to prevent people reserving loungers with towels in the holiday sunbed scramble.

stories.title: His father had just been buried. Then West Bank settlers forced him to dig up the body
stories.summary: The UN human rights office condemns incident as "'appalling and emblematic of the dehumanisation of Palestinians" in the West Bank.

stories.title: Tourist hotspot at 'end of the world' denies causing hantavirus outbreak
stories.summary: The BBC reports from the city of Ushuaia, where experts have been sent to investigate the origins of the outbreak.

stories.title: Iran activists tell BBC how threat of war intensifies trauma of repression
stories.summary: A dissident in Tehran says she feels helpless and under immense psychological pressure.

stories.title: BBC speaks with civilians inside Iran struggling with impact of war
stories.summary: Iranian authorities have imposed an internet ban but using trusted sources, special correspondent Fergal Keane hears from those traumatised by conflict and government repression.

stories.title: Tiborcz, Garancsi és Jellinek körének is jutott a milliárdos állami ingatlanokból
stories.summary: Nem egyértelműen NER-es vagyonkijátszás volt a választások előtti hónapokra időzített nagy állami ingatlankiárusítás, de többmilliárdos ingatlan is kormányközeli üzletemberek környezetében landolt.

stories.title: „Elálltak a vásárlók, mert a lakásommal együtt Lázárt is megveszik” – az exminiszter felvásárlásai aggasztják egy társasház lakóit
stories.summary: Az V. kerületi házban lakó egyik férfi még Ferencz Orsolyához is fordult az ügyben – a képviselő azt mondta, amikor a bukás okait vizsgálják, az ilyen ügyekről is beszélni kell.

stories.title: Egész életemben emlékezni fogok – üzente a brit énekesnő, aki élőben adta elő a Hegedűst táncra perdítő dalát
stories.summary: „Igazi kiváltság, hogy egy kis szerepet játszhattam egy történelmi pillanatban” – írta Jalja.

stories.title: Szükség lehet az esernyőkre hétfőn, jönnek a zivatarok
stories.summary: A hőmérséklet csúcsértéke általában 21 és 26 fok között alakul.

stories.title: A Fidesz olyan volt az önkormányzatokkal, mint egy agresszív kamaszfiú, innen most már csak jobb lehet
stories.summary: A Fidesz 16 év alatt módszeresen leépítette az önkormányzatok mozgásterét, elvonták helyi hatásköreiket és bevételeiket, így látják a helyzetet a Telexnek nyilatkozó polgármesterek. A nagyvárosok fejlődése megtorpant, a falvak nehéz helyzetbe kerültek, a párthűséget finanszírozták, mondta az egyik városvezető. Most a szolidaritási adó átalakítását remélik, de ez sem lesz egyszerű feladat az új kormánynak.

stories.title: Nógrád megyében is tízmilliós támogatások mentek a Fideszhez köthető alapítványokhoz a választás előtt
stories.summary: A salgótarjáni Fidesz elnökének egyesülete csak az NKA-tól 45 millió forintot kapott.

stories.title: Hétfő reggel elkezdődött a Tisza-kormány leendő minisztereinek bizottsági meghallgatása
stories.summary: Tovább pörgetik a kormányalakítást, hétfőn tizenhárom bizottság tart meghallgatást.

stories.title: Trump több mint hat hónap után találkozik újra a kínai elnökkel
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök a héten Pekingbe látogat. Várhatóan Iránról, Tajvanról és a nukleáris fegyverekről is tárgyal majd Hszi Csin-pinggel.

stories.title: Tanács Zoltán: Sulyoknak volt néhány kényelmetlen perce, de mi ez ahhoz képest, amit korábban azok éltek át, akikért a köztársasági elnök nem szólt egy szót se?
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt tudományos és technológiai miniszterjelöltje bizottsági meghallgatásán beszélt az MI-ről, átvilágításokról, digitális fejlesztésekről és a tudósok helyzetének javításáról.

stories.title: A Paks II. szerződéseinek felülvizsgálatát ígérte meg Kapitány István
stories.summary: Az orosz olajról való leválás kapcsán arról beszélt Kapitány, hogy „semmiről nem szeretnénk leválni, hanem opciókat szeretnénk kapni”.

stories.title: Gajdos László Attenborough sorozata után nevezte el az új környezetvédelmi minisztériumot
stories.summary: A miniszterjelölt meghallgatásán arról is beszélt, hogy sokkal jobban bevonná a civileket a környezetvédelmi döntéshozatalba, leállítanák a védett erdőkben a fakitermelést, a tarvágást felfüggesztenék. A madártani egyesület szóvivőjét kérte fel a minisztérium szóvivőjének.

stories.title: Az olaj árát is megemelte, hogy Donald Trumpnak nem tetszett Irán megállapodási javaslata
stories.summary: A brent olaj ára 4,1 százalékkal, míg az amerikai tőzsdén kereskedett nyersolaj ára 4,4 százalékkal emelkedett.

stories.title: Lemondott Bosznia-Hercegovina főképviselője, aki többször is az ország szétesésére figyelmeztetett
stories.summary: A hírek szerint az Egyesült Államok ultimátumot adott Christian Schmidtnek, hogy haladéktalanul távozzon.

stories.title: Tárkányi Zsolt lesz Vitézy parlamenti államtitkára a Közlekedési és Beruházási Minisztériumban
stories.summary: A debreceni képviselő tartja majd a kapcsolatot a Tisza parlamenti frakciójával.

stories.title: Amerikai járványügyi vezető a hantavírussal fertőzött óceánjáróról: Ez nem Covid, nem is akarjuk úgy kezelni
stories.summary: Evakuálják az utasokat az óceánjáróról, hetekig tartó izoláció vár rájuk.

stories.title: Leszállás után evakuálták a Turkish Airlines gépét Nepálban
stories.summary: Gurulás közben tűz ütött ki az Airbus A330-as repülőgép jobb hátsó futóművénél.

stories.title: Vádat emeltek a férfi ellen, aki még márciusban rugdosta tiszás aktivisták pultját
stories.summary: A férfit az zavarta, hogy az általa üzemeltetett üzletsor előtti járdaszakaszon gyűjtöttek aláírást.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt Takács Péternek: Ön az egészségügy Menczer Tamása
stories.summary: „Nem tudom, hogy történt, hogy önnek kígyózó mozdulatokkal sikerült bekerülnie a parlamentbe a lista 123. helyéről” – mondta a Tisza miniszterjelöltje Takácsnak Péternek.

stories.title: Visszafogottabb időszak jöhet az albérletpiacon
stories.summary: Az Ingatlan.com szerint áprilisban stagnáltak az albérletárak, mivel az Otthon Start ősszel rengeteg bérlőt kiszívott a piacról, és már egyébként sem olyan vonzó befektetés a lakáspiac.

stories.title: Gulyás szerint a Fidesz az elhangzottak alapján dönt a miniszterjelöltek alkalmasságáról
stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezetője azt ígérte, hogy pártja „normális ellenzék” lesz.

stories.title: Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz: Nem megyünk Csádba, nem állítjuk vissza a sorkötelezettséget
stories.summary: A honvédelmi miniszterjelölt azt ígérte: visszaállítják a NATO-szövetségesek bizalmát, és helyreállítják a katonaság morálját. Szerinte a Tisza-kormány a béke kormánya lesz, celebtartalékosokra pedig nincs szükség. A KDNP-s Simicskó István azt ígérte: támogatni fogja a honvédelmi minisztert.

stories.title: Tarr: Öt évre visszamenőleg kivizsgáljuk a Bethlen Gábor Alapot, sokszor nem létező egyesületek kaptak pénzt
stories.summary: Tarr Zoltán Lázár szaros-mávos mondatát is idézte a nemzetiségek bizottsági meghallgatásán. A leendő kultúráért felelős miniszter arról beszélt, hogy céljuk a Fidesz által felépített kirekesztő és gyűlöletkeltő rendszert lebontani, mert ez különösen érintette a nemzetiségeket – főként az ukránokat. Azt is ígérte, hogy a nemzetiségi listához tartozó visszaéléseket is ki fogják vizsgálni.

stories.title: Pósfai Gábor: Kivizsgáljuk a Pegasus-ügyet
stories.summary: A Nemzetbiztonsági Bizottság meghallgatásán Pósfai Gábor belügyminiszter-jelölt arról beszélt, hogy nem akar rendőrminiszter lenni, a rendvédelemben a lojalitás nem lehet fontosabb a szakmaiságnál.

stories.title: Dua Lipa beperelte a Samsungot, miután az engedélye nélkül adtak el tévéket az arcával
stories.summary: Az énekesnő 15 millió dollárt követel a cégtől.

stories.title: EU külügyi főképviselő: Gerhard Schröder nem alkalmas arra, hogy Európát képviselje az orosz–ukrán tárgyalásokon
stories.summary: Kaja Kallas szerint egyértelmű, hogy Vlagyimir Putyin miért akarja a tárgyalóasztalnál látni a volt német kancellárt, aki évek óta orosz cégek lobbistája.

stories.title: Pogány Induló színészként debütál A besúgó alkotójának filmjében
stories.summary: Szirmai Marcell a szombati koncertjén árulta el a közönségének a hírt, ahol arról is beszélt, hogy a választás után sem fog megszólalni politikai ügyekben.

stories.title: A Curiosity marsjáró véletlenül kitört egy darabot a Marsból, napokba telt, mire megszabadult tőle
stories.summary: Mintát akart venni egy kőtömbből, de az egész rászorult a fúrást végző robotkarjára.

stories.title: Vitézy: A vasútrombolás korát le kell zárni
stories.summary: Megnyitják a múlt rejtett aktáit, hazahozzák az uniós forrásokat, fellépnek a durva gyorshajtók ellen, vasúti járműfejlesztésbe kezdenek, mondta a közlekedési miniszterjelölt parlamenti meghallgatásán.

stories.title: Zivataros lesz a hétfő délután, erős szél és jégeső is jöhet
stories.summary: Akár a narancssárga riasztás kiadása is indokolttá válhat.

stories.title: Guardian reporter and colleagues detained and beaten by Somali police
stories.summary: Mohamed Bulbul arrested in Mogadishu after covering case of woman allegedly being tortured in prison A journalist who covered the case of a woman who said she was being tortured in prison was detained and beaten with pistols by Somali authorities, along with two others, for his reporting for the Guardian. Mohamed Bulbul was arrested with the journalists Abdihafid Nor Barre and Abdishakur Mohamed Mohamud on Friday evening while in a restaurant in the centre of the Somali capital, Mogadishu. They

stories.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards threaten US sites in Middle East if tankers come under fire
stories.summary: Revolutionary Guards issue warning as Trump awaits Iran’s response to Washington’s latest proposal for peace deal Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have threatened to target US sites in the Middle East if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media reported on Saturday, as Washington was left waiting for Tehran’s response to its latest negotiating position. “Any attack on Iranian tankers and commercial vessels will result in a heavy attack on one of the American centres in the region and enemy ships,”

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: This year's Victory Day parade in Moscow felt very different
stories.summary: The BBC's Russia editor reports from a Red Square with none of the military hardware which the Kremlin normally showcases to project power internationally.

stories.title: At least 39 killed in fresh Israeli strikes on Lebanon
stories.summary: Despite a three-week-old ceasefire, Israel struck Lebanon repeatedly on Saturday killing at least thirty-nine people, while Hezbollah retaliated with drone attacks on northern Israel.

stories.title: Four south Florida men convicted in Haitian president’s assassination
stories.summary: Men were convicted in Miami federal court for plotting to kill Jovenel Moïse at his Port-au-Prince home in 2021 Four south Florida men were convicted on Friday of plotting to kill the Haitian president, Jovenel Moïse , in 2021 by hiring mercenaries to assassinate him at his Port-au-Prince home, court records show. Prosecutors argued during the nine-week trial in a Miami federal court that the men assembled two dozen former Colombian soldiers and supplied them with money, guns, ammunition and tac

stories.title: Why a weakened Starmer will worry the EU
stories.summary: After bruising local election losses, Sir Keir Starmer is preparing a renewed push for closer ties with the European Union — but Brussels worries that his political weakness could stall or derail any meaningful post-Brexit reset.

stories.title: Hantavirus ship nears Tenerife as residents voice safety fears
stories.summary: The Spanish government has authorised passengers from the MV Hondius to disembark at the port of Granadilla this weekend following an agreement with the World Health Organisation. The decision has sparked protests from local dock workers in Santa Cruz.

stories.title: ‘Watching us is like watching a cousin’: the online creators reshaping Africa’s news ecosphere
stories.summary: Africa is leading a change in news consumption habits – and transforming the lives of current affairs enthusiasts Last year Amahle-Imvelo Jaxa posted a TikTok video about South African peacekeepers in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. She explained an argument that had erupted between the South African and Rwandan presidents, then listed roles different South African groups would play in a war with Rwanda: the Sotho strategists, the Xhosa negotiators, the Afrikaner muscle. The video went vir

stories.title: Indonesian rescuers retrieve body from Mount Dukono as search continues
stories.summary: Woman recovered after volcanic eruption on remote island, while operation to find two missing Singaporeans goes on Rescuers have recovered the body of an Indonesian woman who was caught in a volcanic eruption on Mount Dukono on Indonesia’s remote island of Halmahera, officials have said. Search operations continued on Saturday for the bodies of two Singaporeans. The dead hikers were among 20 who set out to scale the 1,355-metre (4,445ft) volcano in defiance of safety restrictions and became stra

stories.title: Argentina in spotlight over hantavirus as authorities retrace footsteps of ship’s passengers
stories.summary: Thirty years after first person-to-person transmission was documented in Patagonia, scientists say global heating could increase world’s exposure An outbreak in rural communities 30 years ago in the Patagonia area of Argentina led scientists, for the first time, to document person-to-person transmission of hantavirus, which until then had been known only to spread through contact with rodents. Nearly a decade ago another outbreak, also in Patagonia, provided detailed evidence of inter-human tran

stories.title: Tehran, Taiwan, trade … what are the hazards facing Trump on Xi summit tightrope?
stories.summary: US leader enters talks with superpower rival from vulnerable position, but will be hoping for economic wins amid turbulent backdrop If all goes to plan over the next few days – and that is a big if – Donald Trump will arrive in Beijing on Wednesday for a highly anticipated summit with Xi Jinping , China’s leader. The trip will mark the first time a US president has visited China in nearly a decade. The last visit was also made by Trump , during his first term, in 2017. Continue reading...

stories.title: Egyik lottón sem volt telitalálat a héten
stories.summary: Pedig majdnem egy hónapja, a választás utáni héten négy lottón is volt olyan játékos, aki minden számot eltalált.

stories.title: Lezárult Felsőszentmártonon az időközi választás, miután az előző testület februárban feloszlott
stories.summary: A két független jelölt közül Várnai Levente nyerte a választást.

stories.title: Brit ejtőernyősök vittek orvosi segítséget egy hantavírus-fertőzöttnek az Egyesült Királyság egyik szigetére
stories.summary: A korábban a MV Hondiuson utazó brit állampolgár oxigénellátásra szorult, és kezdett kifogyni a készletekből.

stories.title: Óriási pofont kapott a Munkáspárt, a brit kormányfő kézzel-lábbal kapaszkodik a székébe
stories.summary: A Munkáspárt csütörtökön az önkormányzatokban, Skóciában és Walesben is egyaránt leszerepelt, de a toryk sem jártak jobban. A vereség újabb szög lehet Keir Starmer koporsójában, az egészből pedig a Farage-féle, a legnépszerűbb párttá vált Reform és a szintén radikális változásokat követelő Zöldek profitálnak.

stories.title: Huszonkilencedik bajnoki címét szerezte meg az FC Barcelona a Real Madrid ellen a spanyol bajnokságon
stories.summary: 2-0-ra nyert a Barcelona csapata.

stories.title: New film portrays Thomas and Erika Mann — with an oddly twisted premise
stories.summary: Premiering at Cannes and starring Sandra Hüller, Pawel Pawlikowski's "Fatherland" is set during the Cold War — a complex period for the prominent anti-Nazi intellectuals.

stories.title: Putin says he thinks Ukraine war is 'coming to an end'
stories.summary: The Russian president made the remarks as Moscow and Kyiv observe a three-day ceasefire in their four-year conflict.

stories.title: Antisemitism in Germany, and the CDU's search for answers
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz's CDU party has been struggling to find the right words on how it intends to protect Jewish life in the capital. A recent declaration does not go far beyond calling for "a clear stance."

stories.title: Pakistan: Car bomb attack at checkpoint kills 15
stories.summary: The attack occurred in Bannu district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, where militants drove an explosives‑laden vehicle into a police checkpoint.

stories.title: Data centers: Critical tech infrastructure under threat
stories.summary: Data centers are energy-intensive engines of growth, the backbone and hub of digitalization. Thousands of them are being built all around the world. The Iran war has shown how vulnerable societies are as a result.

stories.title: The weaponization of shipping channels
stories.summary: The crisis around the Strait of Hormuz focused attention on other maritime chokepoints. Experts warn that waterways like the Taiwan Strait or the Strait of Malacca are increasingly being used as geopolitical leverage.

stories.title: Rosenberg: Scaled-back Victory Parade in Moscow's Red Square
stories.summary: The BBC's Russia editor reports from Moscow, where the annual Victory Day display was quieter than usual.

stories.title: US says the fragile truce with Iran holds as it awaits response to its latest ceasefire proposal
stories.summary: Washington insists its month-long ceasefire with Iran is continuing to hold despite renewed hostilities and the recent exchanges of fire. The US says it is awaiting Iran’s response to its latest ceasefire proposal to end the war.

stories.title: Libya launches major security operation in Zawiya after clashes near key oil refinery
stories.summary: In Zawiya, home to one of the oil-rich country’s biggest refineries, rival gangs clash over the trafficking of fuel and commercial goods into neighbouring Tunisia.

stories.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes killed 39
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to trade fire despite a ceasefire deal being announced last month.

stories.title: Why Canada is seeing its biggest military recruitment surge in 30 years
stories.summary: The country has seen its biggest military recruitment surge in 30 years, a major turnaround after years of falling behind on defence capabilities.

stories.title: Another year, another controversy for Eurovision - but fans are sticking by it
stories.summary: Fans are grappling with how to react to the withdrawal of Spain, Ireland and others over Israel's participation.

stories.title: Indian model's understated Met Gala debut revives debate on cultural representation
stories.summary: Bhavitha Mandava’s Met Gala debut has brought a new wave of attention to her - and to her understated aesthetic.

stories.title: How could extreme weather affect World Cup 2026?
stories.summary: With almost a month to go until the start of the World Cup, Simon King looks ahead to the weather's potential to disrupt the tournament this summer.

stories.title: Buddhist monk arrested over alleged rape of teen in Sri Lanka
stories.summary: The Venerable Pallegama Hemarathana Thero holds one of the most revered positions in the Buddhist world.

stories.title: Suspected boat explosion injures 11 in Miami
stories.summary: Officials are investigating what caused the incident at a popular marina in southern Florida.

stories.title: Hantavirus ship passengers deemed high-risk as new case emerges in Tenerife evacuation
stories.summary: The hantavirus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius docked in Spain's Canary Islands on Sunday, where passengers will be evacuated on repatriation flights.

stories.title: Putin claims Ukraine war is coming to an end, says he’s open to talks through Europe
stories.summary: Putin declared the war in Ukraine was "heading to an end" on Saturday, even though the first day of a US-brokered ceasefire was marred by mutual accusations of violations and a scaled-down Victory Day parade.

stories.title: Frustrated by Iran, Trump at last seizes enriched uranium – but from Venezuela
stories.summary: US energy department says 13.5kg of uranium taken from reactor in Caracas – a fraction of the 408kg held by Tehran Donald Trump has succeeded in removing a country’s stash of highly enriched uranium – although that country is not Iran . On Friday, the US Department of Energy announced that “thanks to President Trump’s decisive leadership” 13.5kg (about 30 pounds) of uranium had been removed from a legacy research reactor in Venezuela . Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's sugar tax sparks 'nanny state' debate
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz's government has agreed to introduce a sugar levy on soft drinks as part of its health care reforms. Critics have called it disproportionate interference, but dozens of countries already have such a levy.

stories.title: Who is coming to Putin’s Victory Day parade — and who is not?
stories.summary: For the Russian president, Victory Day is arguably the most important public holiday of the year. But this time, even Russia’s former allies are staying away, leaving the Kremlin alone in its much scaled-down celebration.

stories.title: Iraq announces huge oil find near Saudi border as Hormuz crisis bites
stories.summary: Iraq has the fifth largest proven oil reserves in the world, estimated at 145 billion barrels, representing about 17% of total Middle Eastern reserves and around 9% of global reserves, according to the US Energy Information Administration (EIA).

stories.title: Putin vows victory in Ukraine and targets NATO at Moscow's scaled-back parade
stories.summary: In Moscow, a scaled-down Victory Day parade was held under tight security, without military hardware, amid fears of possible Ukrainian drone attacks. The Russian president denounced NATO in his speech.

stories.title: Three hikers dead after Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano erupts
stories.summary: Two Singaporeans and a local person were in no-go zone when they were killed, say officials Three hikers – two Singaporeans and an Indonesian – have died in an eruption of Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano, where they found themselves in a no-go zone, officials said. The eruption, on Halmahera island, sent an ash cloud about 6 miles (10km) into the air, with no towns or villages near enough to face any immediate threat. Continue reading...

stories.title: Future of UK lies in the EU after Brexit ‘failure’, say European Greens
stories.summary: Exclusive: Co-chair Vula Tsetsi says it is time to trigger debate, as statement is agreed at annual leadership meeting in Brussels The European Green party has urged the UK to consider rejoining the EU and draw a line under the “political and economic failure” of Brexit. A text declaring that “the United Kingdom’s future lies in the European Union” was adopted by a large majority of European green parties at the movement’s annual leadership meeting on Friday, the day before Europe Day. Continue

stories.title: Iran war costs Toyota £3bn as prices of materials soar and sales fall
stories.summary: Carmaker gives one of biggest warnings yet of conflict’s impact on businesses while Trump tariffs also take toll Toyota has reported a £3bn hit from costs from the war in Iran, as prices of parts and materials soared and sales dropped. The world’s biggest carmaker said profits declined in its financial year to March as it was “likely unable to absorb newly added impact from the Middle East”, in one of the largest warnings yet of the war’s impact on businesses. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian high school where deadly mass shooting occurred to be torn down
stories.summary: Tumbler Ridge secondary school was site of February mass shooting in which nine were killed and dozens injured The school that was the site of one of Canada’s deadliest mass shootings will be torn down, officials have announced. The decision to demolish the Tumbler Ridge secondary school came after meetings between the school board and survivors, family and community members, said the British Columbia premier, David Eby. Continue reading...

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Femen and Pussy Riot protest in Venice, Israeli strikes in Gaza, the hantavirus outbreak and Emma Chamberlain at the Met Gala – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: AI will make language barriers disappear – and diminish our understanding of other cultures
stories.summary: Machines may soon translate every conversation flawlessly. But language is more than information – it is curiosity, intimacy and cultural discovery One of my earliest assignments as a young interpreter was to provide simultaneous interpretation for the proceedings of an ecumenical council that brought together all Christian denominations. As my homework, I dutifully read scripture, the gospels, papal encyclicals and the conclusion of the first council of Nicaea. There was, however, one thing I h

stories.title: Germany's far-right AfD benefits from discontent with Merz
stories.summary: Never before has a German government been this unpopular after only one year in office as that of Chancellor Friedrich Merz. The far-right AfD is capitalizing on this.

stories.title: Why Russia has scaled back May 9 Victory Parade
stories.summary: This year's May 9 military parades in Moscow will be much more modest and have been canceled altogether in other parts of Russia. The Kremlin has blamed Ukraine.

stories.title: How real is a coup threat against Russia's president?
stories.summary: Increased security around Vladimir Putin and a scaling back of public appearances have given rise to suspicions of a coup or assassination attempt. Yet some observers remain skeptical.

stories.title: Trump announces 3-day ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia
stories.summary: The US president said both sides had agreed to halt fighting from May 9 to 11 and to exchange 1,000 prisoners each. Both Ukraine and Russia confirmed the ceasefire and prisoner exchange. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Eurovision 2026: Politics collide with a blockbuster show
stories.summary: Controversy over Israel's participation and boycotts unprecedented in scale are already weighing on the Eurovision Song Contest before it even kicks off in Vienna.

stories.title: Vigils held across Australia after death of 5-year-old girl
stories.summary: People across Australia held candlelight vigils to remember a 5-year-old girl whose death has shocked the nation.

stories.title: Portuguese man's body found inside crocodile in South Africa
stories.summary: South African authorities shot the animal and hoisted it from a river in order to recover the man's remains. The identity of the man, a Portuguese businessman, was confirmed by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

stories.title: Brown bears rescued from harsh captivity conditions in Armenia
stories.summary: Bears rescued from captivity in Armenia are recovering at a wildlife refuge after being kept in poor conditions as status symbols.

stories.title: Indonesia volcano eruption kills three hikers on Mount Dukono
stories.summary: Mount Dukono erupted in North Halmahera, Indonesia, sending a massive ash cloud 10 kilometres into the sky. Three hikers, including two foreigners, were killed and 10 remain missing. Rescue teams are searching for 20 people affected near the volcano, officials said.

stories.title: Huge plumes of ash rise as Indonesia's Mount Dukono erupts
stories.summary: Officials said the group had been hiking up the active volcano despite a climbing ban.

stories.title: Return of Palestine Marathon attracts thousands of runners
stories.summary: Thousands of people have taken part in the Palestine Marathon, which returns to Bethlehem after a two-year pause over the Gaza War.

stories.title: Return of IS-linked families sparks debate in terror-traumatised Australia
stories.summary: Most Australians do not want the women and children to come home, but advocates say they're in danger.

stories.title: Move over matcha. Hojicha is coming to a cafe near you
stories.summary: From lattes to ice-cream, the Japanese tea is popping up more and more on menus across the UK.

stories.title: Anti-work anthems, Boy George and controversial lyrics: A guide to all 35 Eurovision songs
stories.summary: From dancefloor bangers to heartfelt ballads, here are the highlights and low points of Eurovision.

stories.title: Syria's Jewish community in Damascus wants to keep kosher food on the menu
stories.summary: Syria's small Jewish community has struggled to maintain kosher traditions after it dwindled in size, relying on imported meat and individual effort to keep centuries-old practices alive in Damascus.
